steps.title: Identify what needs to be compared
steps.content: The goal is to compare disadvantages—what makes each system harder to use or deploy? From the notes: Wayang Windu's flash steam system needs reservoirs above 180℃ (limiting its applicability since many reservoirs aren't hot enough); Raft River's binary cycle system costs more to maintain. To accomplish the goal, a choice must address both disadvantages, not just one.

steps.title: Match the comparison structure
steps.content:
Choice D states that the Wayang Windu system requires temperatures above 180℃ while the Raft River system can operate at lower temperatures, directly comparing the two plants' disadvantages with respect to temperature requirements. The other choices either name only one plant's drawback or fail to put both plants' disadvantages side by side.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
